# Agentic Workflow Examples

This guide demonstrates advanced agentic capabilities where the AI autonomously uses multiple tools to accomplish complex tasks.

## 🤖 Understanding Agentic Behavior

In agentic mode, the AI can:
- Chain multiple tool calls together
- Make decisions about which tools to use
- Iterate on solutions based on results
- Handle errors and retry with different approaches

## 🔧 Setup for Agentic Workflows

```bash
# Enable autonomous mode for smoother workflows
> /mode autonomous

# Enable debug logging to see tool execution
> /set logLevel debug

# Increase tool call limit for complex tasks
> /set maxToolCalls 15
```

## 🏗️ Complete Project Creation

### Build a Todo App

```bash
> Build me a complete React todo application with local storage, filtering, and responsive design

# AI will autonomously:
# 1. Plan the project structure
# 2. Create package.json with dependencies
# 3. Create React components (App, TodoList, TodoItem, AddTodo)
# 4. Create CSS files with responsive design
# 5. Implement local storage functionality
# 6. Add filtering capabilities (all, active, completed)
# 7. Create README with setup instructions

Expected tool sequence:
🔧 create_directory: todo-app
🔧 create_multiple_files: [package.json, src/App.js, src/components/*, src/styles/*]
🔧 write_file: README.md
```

### Build an Express API

```bash
> Create a Node.js REST API for a blog with authentication, CRUD operations, and MongoDB integration

# AI will autonomously:
# 1. Create project structure
# 2. Set up Express server with middleware
# 3. Create authentication routes and JWT handling
# 4. Implement blog post CRUD operations
# 5. Add MongoDB models and connection
# 6. Create middleware for auth and validation
# 7. Add error handling and logging
# 8. Create API documentation

Expected tool sequence:
🔧 create_directory: blog-api
🔧 create_multiple_files: [package.json, server.js, routes/*, models/*, middleware/*]
🔧 write_file: .env.example
🔧 write_file: README.md
```

## 🔍 Automated Code Analysis

### Security Audit

```bash
> Perform a comprehensive security audit of my application and fix any vulnerabilities you find

# AI will autonomously:
# 1. Scan all source files for security issues
# 2. Check for common vulnerabilities (XSS, SQL injection, etc.)
# 3. Review authentication and authorization
# 4. Check for exposed secrets or API keys
# 5. Fix identified issues
# 6. Generate security report

Expected tool sequence:
🔧 list_directory: src/
🔧 read_file: src/auth/login.js
🔧 search_code: "password.*plain"
🔧 search_code: "eval\("
🔧 replace_in_file: Fix SQL injection
🔧 write_file: SECURITY_REPORT.md
```

### Performance Optimization

```bash
> Analyze my React application for performance issues and implement optimizations

# AI will autonomously:
# 1. Analyze component structure and re-renders
# 2. Check for memory leaks and inefficient hooks
# 3. Review bundle size and imports
# 4. Implement React.memo, useMemo, useCallback where needed
# 5. Optimize images and assets
# 6. Add performance monitoring

Expected tool sequence:
🔧 search_code: "useEffect.*\[\]"
🔧 read_file: src/components/ExpensiveComponent.jsx
🔧 replace_in_file: Add React.memo
🔧 write_file: src/hooks/useOptimizedData.js
🔧 write_file: PERFORMANCE_REPORT.md
```

## 🐛 Automated Debugging

### Fix Test Failures

```bash
> Run the test suite and fix any failing tests

# AI will autonomously:
# 1. Execute test command
# 2. Analyze test failures
# 3. Read failing test files
# 4. Identify root causes
# 5. Fix the underlying code issues
# 6. Re-run tests to verify fixes

Expected tool sequence:
🔧 execute_command: npm test
🔧 read_file: tests/user.test.js
🔧 read_file: src/models/User.js
🔧 replace_in_file: Fix validation logic
🔧 execute_command: npm test
```

### Debug Production Issues

```bash
> I'm getting 500 errors in production. Can you help debug and fix the issue?

# AI will autonomously:
# 1. Check error logs
# 2. Analyze server code for potential issues
# 3. Review database connections and queries
# 4. Check environment configuration
# 5. Implement fixes and error handling
# 6. Add monitoring and logging

Expected tool sequence:
🔧 read_file: logs/error.log
🔧 search_code: "500.*error"
🔧 read_file: src/routes/api.js
🔧 replace_in_file: Add error handling
🔧 write_file: src/middleware/errorHandler.js
```

## 📚 Documentation Generation

### API Documentation

```bash
> Generate comprehensive API documentation for my Express application

# AI will autonomously:
# 1. Scan all route files
# 2. Extract endpoints, methods, and parameters
# 3. Generate OpenAPI/Swagger specification
# 4. Create markdown documentation
# 5. Add example requests and responses
# 6. Set up documentation hosting

Expected tool sequence:
🔧 list_directory: src/routes/
🔧 read_file: src/routes/users.js
🔧 read_file: src/routes/posts.js
🔧 write_file: swagger.yaml
🔧 write_file: docs/API.md
🔧 create_multiple_files: [docs/examples/*, postman/collection.json]
```

### Code Documentation

```bash
> Add comprehensive JSDoc comments to all my JavaScript files and generate documentation

# AI will autonomously:
# 1. Scan all JavaScript files
# 2. Analyze function signatures and purposes
# 3. Add JSDoc comments with proper types
# 4. Generate HTML documentation
# 5. Create documentation index

Expected tool sequence:
🔧 search_code: "function.*\(" --type js
🔧 read_file: src/utils/helpers.js
🔧 replace_in_file: Add JSDoc comments
🔧 execute_command: npx jsdoc src/ -d docs/
🔧 write_file: docs/README.md
```

## 🔄 Refactoring Workflows

### Modernize Legacy Code

```bash
> Modernize this jQuery codebase to use modern vanilla JavaScript and ES6+ features

# AI will autonomously:
# 1. Analyze existing jQuery code
# 2. Identify jQuery dependencies
# 3. Convert to modern JavaScript
# 4. Update event handling and DOM manipulation
# 5. Add ES6+ features (arrow functions, destructuring, etc.)
# 6. Update build process

Expected tool sequence:
🔧 search_code: "\$\(" --regex
🔧 read_file: src/legacy/main.js
🔧 replace_in_file: Convert jQuery to vanilla JS
🔧 write_file: src/modern/main.js
🔧 replace_in_file: Update package.json
```

### Convert to TypeScript

```bash
> Convert my JavaScript React application to TypeScript

# AI will autonomously:
# 1. Add TypeScript configuration
# 2. Install TypeScript dependencies
# 3. Convert JavaScript files to TypeScript
# 4. Add type definitions for props and state
# 5. Fix type errors
# 6. Update build scripts

Expected tool sequence:
🔧 write_file: tsconfig.json
🔧 replace_in_file: Update package.json dependencies
🔧 read_file: src/App.js
🔧 write_file: src/App.tsx
🔧 replace_in_file: Add TypeScript types
```

## 🧪 Testing Automation

### Generate Test Suite

```bash
> Create a comprehensive test suite for my application with unit, integration, and e2e tests

# AI will autonomously:
# 1. Analyze application structure
# 2. Create Jest configuration
# 3. Generate unit tests for all functions
# 4. Create integration tests for API endpoints
# 5. Set up e2e tests with Playwright
# 6. Add test coverage reporting

Expected tool sequence:
🔧 write_file: jest.config.js
🔧 create_multiple_files: [tests/unit/*, tests/integration/*, tests/e2e/*]
🔧 write_file: playwright.config.js
🔧 replace_in_file: Add test scripts to package.json
```

### Fix Test Coverage

```bash
> Analyze test coverage and add tests to reach 90% coverage

# AI will autonomously:
# 1. Run coverage report
# 2. Identify uncovered code
# 3. Generate tests for uncovered functions
# 4. Add edge case tests
# 5. Verify coverage improvement

Expected tool sequence:
🔧 execute_command: npm run test:coverage
🔧 read_file: coverage/lcov-report/index.html
🔧 search_code: "function.*uncovered"
🔧 write_file: tests/unit/uncovered.test.js
🔧 execute_command: npm run test:coverage
```

## 🚀 Deployment Automation

### Setup CI/CD Pipeline

```bash
> Set up a complete CI/CD pipeline with GitHub Actions for testing, building, and deployment

# AI will autonomously:
# 1. Create GitHub Actions workflow
# 2. Set up testing pipeline
# 3. Configure build and deployment
# 4. Add environment-specific configurations
# 5. Set up monitoring and notifications

Expected tool sequence:
🔧 create_directory: .github/workflows/
🔧 write_file: .github/workflows/ci.yml
🔧 write_file: .github/workflows/deploy.yml
🔧 write_file: docker-compose.yml
🔧 write_file: Dockerfile
```

### Environment Setup

```bash
> Set up development, staging, and production environments with proper configuration management

# AI will autonomously:
# 1. Create environment-specific config files
# 2. Set up Docker containers
# 3. Configure database connections
# 4. Set up environment variables
# 5. Create deployment scripts

Expected tool sequence:
🔧 create_multiple_files: [.env.development, .env.staging, .env.production]
🔧 write_file: docker-compose.dev.yml
🔧 write_file: scripts/deploy.sh
🔧 write_file: k8s/deployment.yaml
```

## 🔄 Multi-Step Problem Solving

### Complex Bug Investigation

```bash
> Users are reporting intermittent 500 errors. Investigate and fix the root cause.

# AI will autonomously:
# 1. Check error logs and patterns
# 2. Analyze server code for race conditions
# 3. Review database queries and connections
# 4. Check memory usage and performance
# 5. Implement fixes and monitoring
# 6. Add preventive measures

Expected tool sequence:
🔧 read_file: logs/error.log
🔧 search_code: "async.*await" --type js
🔧 read_file: src/database/connection.js
🔧 replace_in_file: Add connection pooling
🔧 write_file: src/middleware/rateLimiter.js
🔧 write_file: monitoring/healthcheck.js
```

### Feature Implementation

```bash
> Add user authentication with OAuth, password reset, and email verification to my application

# AI will autonomously:
# 1. Plan authentication architecture
# 2. Set up OAuth providers (Google, GitHub)
# 3. Implement JWT token handling
# 4. Create password reset flow
# 5. Add email verification system
# 6. Update frontend components
# 7. Add security middleware

Expected tool sequence:
🔧 write_file: src/auth/oauth.js
🔧 write_file: src/auth/jwt.js
🔧 write_file: src/auth/passwordReset.js
🔧 write_file: src/auth/emailVerification.js
🔧 create_multiple_files: [src/components/Login.jsx, src/components/Register.jsx]
🔧 replace_in_file: Add auth routes to app.js
```

## 📊 Monitoring Tool Execution

### Understanding the Agent Loop

Watch for these patterns in agentic workflows:

```
🔧 Tool: list_directory
📁 Result: Found 15 files in src/

🔧 Tool: read_file  
📄 Result: Read src/app.js (2.3KB)

🤖 Thinking: I need to check the database connection...

🔧 Tool: search_code
🔍 Result: Found 3 matches for "database"

🔧 Tool: write_file
✅ Result: Created src/database/pool.js

🤖 Final: I've implemented connection pooling to fix the 500 errors.
```

### Debugging Agent Behavior

```bash
# Enable verbose logging to see AI reasoning
> /set logLevel debug

# Watch for tool execution patterns
# - Sequential: One tool after another
# - Conditional: Tools based on previous results  
# - Iterative: Retry with different approaches
# - Parallel: Multiple related operations

# If agent gets stuck, you can intervene:
> Stop and let me review what you've done so far
> Try a different approach
> Focus only on the authentication part
```

## 🎯 Best Practices for Agentic Workflows

### Effective Task Description

```bash
# ✅ Good - Clear goal and context
> Build a complete e-commerce checkout flow with payment processing, inventory management, and order confirmation emails

# ✅ Good - Specific requirements
> Refactor the user authentication system to use JWT tokens, add password strength validation, and implement rate limiting

# ❌ Avoid - Too vague
> Make my app better

# ❌ Avoid - Too restrictive
> Only modify line 23 in auth.js to add one semicolon
```

### Managing Complex Workflows

```bash
# Break down large tasks
> First, analyze the current authentication system
> Now implement JWT token handling
> Next, add password reset functionality
> Finally, update the frontend components

# Provide feedback during execution
> That looks good, but can you also add input validation?
> The database connection approach won't work in our environment, try Redis instead
```

### Error Recovery

```bash
# If something goes wrong:
> Stop the current task and show me what you've created so far
> Undo the last change and try a different approach
> The test is failing, can you debug and fix it?

# Reset if needed:
> /clear
> Let's start over with a simpler approach
```
